properties.remark: HEAVY RAIN OVERNIGHT CAUSED A MUD/DEBRIS/ROCK FLOW ACROSS HIGHWAY 89A AT MILE POST 379. GUARD RAIL AND MILE POST SIGN WASHED AWAY. SNOWPLOW CLEARED ROAD. (EST TIME).
MESONET STATION 2 SW CORDES LAKES /D9216/ RESIDENT REPORTS TWO SMALL TREES UPROOTED AND FLAGPOLE SNAPPED.
INCREASED FLOW IN BONITA CREEK AS A RESULT OF RAIN ON THE HIGHLINE FIRE SCAR AS WELL AS HEAVY RAIN NEAR BONITA CREEK ESTATES. WATER REMAINED WITHIN BANKS AND DID NOT AFFECT HOMES. TIME ESTIMATED.
CEDAR CREEK FLOWING AT 8 FT AT THE HIGHWAY 73 BRIDGE AT 4:20 PM. REPORTED BY THE WHITE MOUNTAIN APACHE EM.
DEBRIS FROM GOODWIN FIRE FLOWED ACROSS ROAD AT ABOUT 7:00 PM MST.
GRAPEVINE CREEK WITHIN THE GOODWIN FIRE SCAR REPORTED TO BE RUNNING DANGEROUSLY HIGH. RELAYED BY YAVAPAI COUNTY FLOOD CONTROL DISTRICT.
ABOUT 2 FEET OF DEBRIS FLOW WAS OBSERVED ON BIG BUG CREEK WHICH WAS PREVIOUSLY DRY. REPORTED BY MARK MASSIS OF YAVAPAI COUNTY FLOOD CONTROL.
THE BLACK CANYON RIVER WASH IS RUNNING WITH BLACK WATER. THIS WASH IS NORMALLY DRY.
CENTRAL AVENUE IN MAYER WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING OF BIG BUG CREEK. PHOTOS SHOWED MUD AND DEBRIS OVER THE ROADWAY.
POWER POLES DOWN ALONG CASTLE CREEK NORTH OF LAKE PLEASANT. REPORTED VIA APS WEB OUTAGE MAP.
REPORTED FROM NWS FLAGSTAFF FACEBOOK PAGE. TIME ESTIMATED USING RADAR.
PINE TREES BENT OVER SIDEWAYS, DAMAGE TO MANZANITA AND SCRUB OAK TREES. TIME ESTIMATED.
3/4 HAIL.

*** 10 FATAL *** FLASH FLOODING REPORTED NEAR THE JUNCTION OF THE EAST VERDE RIVER AND ELLISON CREEK IN NORTHERN GILA COUNTY AZ. SWIFT WATER RESCUE TEAMS RESCUED 4 PEOPLE. 10 PEOPLE ARE CONFIRMED DEAD.

FLASH FLOODING REPORTED NEAR THE JUNCTION OF THE EAST VERDE RIVER AND ELLISON CREEK IN NORTHERN GILA COUNTY AZ. SWIFT WATER RESCUE TEAMS RESCUED 4 PEOPLE. 4 PEOLE ARE CONFIRMED DEAD AND 6 ARE STILL MISSING. THIS INFORMATION IS ONLY PRELIMINARY. NWS/FLAGSTAFF WILL UPDATES THIS REPORT WHEN IT RECEIVES MORE INFORMATION.

MEASURED AT HUMBUG CREEK RAWS. TIME ESTIMATED.
YAVAPAI FLOOD CONTROL REPORTED FAIN LAKE OVERFLOWING AND CAUSING FLOODING IN LYNX CREEK.
JUST BEGAN HAILING WITH LARGEST STONES UP TO QUARTER SIZED.
HAIL SIZE: QUARTER - 1.00 WIND DAMAGE: A JEEP WAS FORCED OFF THE ROAD AND HIT A TELEPHONE POLE.
NAVAJO COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE DISPATCH RECEIVED A PUBLIC REPORT OF FLASH FLOODING OF THE SCOTT WASH OVER ARIZONA STATE ROUTE 377 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 7 AND 8.
RAWS SITE TR489.
FIVE MILES NORTH OF IRON SPRINGS ROAD ON WILLIAMSON VALLEY RD. -- GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL.

APPROXIMATELY 230 PM MONDAY, STRONG WINDS TORE A 24'X72' METAL ROOF FROM A SHED. BASED ON A PHOTO OF THE DAMAGE, WINDS ESTIMATED AT ABOUT 50-55 MPH. IT TORE THE [METAL] SHEETS LIKE PAPER, AND SPLIT AND BROKE THE 2X6S.
POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN.
BIG CHINO WASH ROSE 3 FEET IN A SHORT TIME AT HIGHWAY 89.
RUSSELL WASH FLOODING IN LAKE MONTEZUMA.
COCORAHS REPORT OF FLASH FLOODING NEARBY. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.
RAPID 4 FOOT RISE ON AGUA FRIA RIVER AT HUMBOLDT.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS FLOODED ROADWAY AT FREEMAN HOLLOW CLOSED AND MANHOLE COVERS COMING UP. PUBLIC REPORTS FLOOD WATERS ENTERED PROPERTY.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS BOULDERS ON 89A. WATER DEPTH UNKNOWN. SPOTTER REPORTS FLOODED ROADWAYS 89A AND DRY CREEK RD 6-8 IN ESTIMATED IN ROAD.
ROADS AT WILLOW CREEK RD AND COMMERCE FLOODED. 1 TRUCK STUCK IN WATER.
3 FEET OF WATER AND DEBRIS OVER ROAD. IN DIAMOND VALLEY SUBDIVISION AT EMERALD DR.
YAVAPAI COUNTY EM SOCIAL MEDIA PAGE SHOWED AT LEAST A 2 FEET RISE IN WATER LEVEL OF THE ALBERSON WASH UPSTREAM FROM FAIN LAKE AND LYNX CREEK.
PUBLIC REPORTS QUARTER SIZE HAIL. STORM TOTAL .75 IN OF RAIN.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTS FLOODING ALONG BIG BUG CREEK. EVACUATIONS WERE MADE IN THE CITY OF MAYER.
FLASH FLOOD PHOTO POSTED TO YAVAPAI COUNTY SOCIAL MEDIA PAGE AT INTERSECTION OF CENTRAL AVE. AND BIG BUG CREEK IN MAYER. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.
STREAM GAGE AFMA3 (AGUA FRIA NEAR MAYER) REPORTED A 5.84 FT. RISE IN WATER LEVEL IN A 45-MINUTE PERIOD FROM 8:30PM TO 9:15PM MST.
AN ESTIMATED 2-3 FT. RISE IN THE DINNEBITO WASH ALONG NAVAJO ROUTE 41. PICTURE FROM SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.
BIG BUG CREEK ROSE 2 TO 2.5 FEET AT THE CENTRAL AVE BRIDGE IN MAYER. WIDTH OF FLOOD WATERS WAS ABOUT 50 FEET.
EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED AN 8 FOOT RISE ON CEDAR CREEK AT THE HIGHWAY 73 BRIDGE.
WIND DAMAGE: TREE TRUNK GREATER THAN 12 IN DIAMETER SNAPPED IN FRONT OF THE CLEMENCEAU HERITAGE MUSEUM. WIND SPEED ESTIMATED 55 MPH. HAIL: PENNY SIZE - 0.75 HEAVY RAIN: 1.00-1.24 IN 30 MINUTES.
CARRIZO CREEK PEAKED AT 10 FT. BY 330 PM MST THE CREEK WAS DOWN TO 5 FEET.
CEDAR CREEK PEAKED AT 6 FEET AND WAS RECEDING AT 3:30 PM MST. REPORTED THAT ONE PROPERTY WAS FLOODED; NO REPORTED DAMAGE TO THE HOUSE.
WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH A WET MICROBURST. A FEW AIRCRAFT WERE MOVED/TURNED BY THE WIND. NO DAMAGE REPORTED. THE DURATION OF THE TSTM WND GST EVENT WAS 30 MINUTES.
WIND GUST ESTIMATED AT 50 MPH. AWNING AND TREE DAMAGE AT RANCHO VERDE RV PARK.
FLASH FLOODING ON SCHNEBLY HILL ROAD A FEW MILES FROM 179. PEOPLE FLED CAR UP THE HILL TO ESCAPE.
EXTREMELY HEAVY RAIN JUST STARTED AND THERE IS 5 OF WATER FLOWING THROUGH THE PARKING LOT LIKE A RIVER CARRYING DEBRIS (TRASH, STICKS). THE DURATION OF THE FLASH FLOOD EVENT WAS 2 MINUTES.
BALDWIN LOOP TRAIL DOWN TO VERDE SCHOOL FLOODED. THE ROAD IS FLOWING LIKE A RIVER.
BELL ROCK TRAIL HEAD WASHED OUT. PEOPLE STRANDED ON THE TRAIL. NO INJURIES REPORTED.

1.0 RAIN FELL IN 30 TO 45 MINUTES. PEA SIZED HAIL. WIND GUST TO 47 MPH. SOME MINOR FLOODING OBSERVED IN THE AREA. A LOT OF DEBRIS (ROCKS AND TREE BRANCHES) ON PAGE SPRINGS RD. THE DURATION OF THE FLASH FLOOD EVENT WAS 45 MINUTES.

1.46 INCHES RAIN FELL IN A SHORT TIME. VISIBILITY WAS <1/4 MILE. DITCHES RUNNING LEVEL WITH ROAD. WASH BEHIND HOUSE IS RUNNING FULL (DEPTH UNKNOWN). 8 INCHES IN THE BACK YARD AND IS NOT GOING DOWN. THE DURATION OF THE FLASH FLOOD EVENT WAS 45 MINUTES.
WATER IS TWO FEET OVER THE SPRING LANE BRIDGE CROSSING BIG BUG CREEK.
DRY BEAVER CREEK RIVER GUAGE ROSE FROM 0.95 AT 330 PM MST FT TO 3.74 FT (PEAK) IN 1.25 HRS. AT 545 PM MST THE GUAGE WAS DOWN TO 3.08 FT. THE DURATION OF THE FLASH FLOOD EVENT WAS 1 HOUR 15 MINUTES.
AGUA FRIA NEAR HUMBOLDT ROSE FROM 1.46 FT AT 415 PM MST TO 4.12 FT (PEAK) AT 515 PM MST.
AGUA FRIA NEAR HUMBOLDT ROSE FROM 1.46 FT TO 4.12 FT (PEAK) IN ONE HOUR.
VERDE RIVER NEAR CAMP VERDE ROSE FROM 4.01 FT AT 345 PM MST TO 10.39 FT (PEAK) AT 445 PM MST; ROSE FROM 6.25 FT TO 10 .39 FT IN THE LAST 15 MINUTES (430 PM TO 445 PM).
BLACK CANYON RIVER WASH IS 3'-4' DEEP OVER MAREN AVE. AND IS CURRENTLY IMPASSABLE. THE WATER CAME UP ABOUT 9:30 PM MST.
NEARBY RIVER GAGE (AFRA3), SHOWED A RISE FROM 5.26 FEET TO 10.40 FEET IN 15 MINUTES. THIS REPRESENTS AN INCREASE OF 449 CFS TO 3,545 CFS.
RIVER GAUGE ON THE PARIA RIVER AT LEES FERRY MEASURED A STAGE OF 6.16 FEET INDICATING BETWEEN A 1.5 TO 2 FOOT RISE.
WHITE MOUNTAIN APACHE EM OBSERVED 5 FT OF WATER IN CEDAR CREEK WITH AN INCREASE NOTABLE OVER THE COURSE OF 10 MINUTES. CREEK WAS STILL RISING. CARRIZO WASH HAD 2.5 FT OF FLOWING WATER.
MARTINEZ WASH GAGE ROSE 2.2 FEET WITH 1730 CFS MARICOPA FLOOD DISTRICT.
YAVAPAI FLOOD DISTRICT OFFICIAL REPORTS ONE FOOT OF WATER OVER AREA ROADS DUE TO HEAVY RAIN.
WATER FLOWING INTO LOWER ANTELOPE CANYON.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED PONDING AND RUNNING WATER IN STREETS NEAR YAVAPAI COLLEGE. PEA SIZE HAIL FOR 15 MIN.
USGS GAGE FOR THE GRANITE CREEK AT PRESCOTT (GCRA3) SHOWED A 4.42 FT. RISE IN 30 MINUTES (FROM 11:15AM TO 11:45AM).
BIG BUG CREEK AT SR 69 STREAM GAUGE REPORTED A PEAK STAGE OF 5.1 FEET. THIS IS AN ESTIMATED 4 FOOT RISE.
SEDONA FIRE COULD NOT RESPOND TO AN EMERGENCY DUE TO WATER FLOWING OVER VERDE VALLEY SCHOOL ROAD. AT LEAST TWO FEET OF WATER WAS FLOWING OVER THE ROADWAY.
IMPASSABLE ROADS DUE TO FLOODING AT SCHOOL HOUSE WASH AND BEAVER CREEK RD.
BEAVER CREEK STREAM GAUGE INDICATED A FLASH FLOOD WITH A SUDDEN RISE OF 3 FEET.
FLASH FLOOD WAVE MOVED THROUGH PARIA RIVER. TIME ESTIMATED FROM NEARBY STREAM GUAGE DATA.
PARIA RIVER GAUGE AT LEE'S FERRY INDICATED A RAPID RISE OF 2 FEET.
FLASH FLOOD AT SHADOW RIM GIRL SCOUT CAMP. CHASE CREEK 6-8 FT HIGH AND OVER BANKS. WATER LINES DAMAGED AND ROADS WASHED OUT. 2.45 OF RAIN MEASURED IN LESS THAN 90 MINUTES.
FLASH FLOODING AT THE WATER WHEEL ON HOUSTON MESA ROAD. ROAD IS IMPASSABLE FROM WATER AND HEAVY DEBRIS.
EAST VERDE RIVER IS FLASH FLOODING. IT IS CURRENTLY ABOUT 5-6 FEET DEEP. SPOTTER CLAIMS IT IS THE WORST THEY HAVE SEEN IN MANY YEARS.
EAST VERDE RIVER IS APPROXIMATELY 2 FEET DEEP AT THIS TIME.
THE EAST VERDE RIVER IS NOW RUNNING ABOUT 5-6 FEET DEEP.
ADOT REPORTED HIGHWAY 163 BETWEEN MILE POSTS 399 AND 400 NORTH OF KAYENTA IS FLOODED.
USGS BEAVER CREEK AT MONTEZUMA CASTLE STREAM GAGE REPORTED A 3.36 FT. RISE IN THE LAST HOUR AND A HALF.
SUPAI POLICE DEPARTMENT IS TRACKING A FLASH FLOOD MOVING DOWN HAVASU CREEK. USGS RIVER GAUGE INITIALLY UP 1.6 FT AT 500 PM.
VERY HEAVY RAIN AND DEFINITELY QUARTER SIZED HAIL WITH SOME LARGER.
COTTONWOOD PD REPORTING FLOODING IN HOMES IN SEVERAL AREAS.

POWER LINES KNOCKED DOWN IN STORM ON SYCAMORE CANYON ROAD NEAR TUZIGOOT NM.
THE NORMALLY DRY SILVER CREEK WASH HAD ABOUT A 6 FT. RISE OF WATER. VIDEO REPORT SUBMITTED VIA EMAIL.
MULTIPLE HOMES IN THE CHAPMAN VILLAGE DAMAGED BY DEBRIS AND FALLEN TREES. TWO POLES IN THE VILLAGE WERE DESTROYED WHEN TREES FELL ON THEM AND CAUSED A LOSS OF POWER TO THE AREA.
PAGE AIRPORT HANGAR DAMAGED. JET CENTER WINDOWS WERE BLOWN OUT. A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED IN PAGE. PAGE ASOS RECORDED A PEAK WIND GUST OF 56 MPH AT 12:38 PM MST.
MUD AND ROCKS FLOWING OVER NAVAJO RTE. 15 BETWEEN GREASEWOOD AND GANADO. PICTURES FROM SOCIAL MEDIA PAGE.
